body_txt: When it comes to angels, they can come in a lot of different forms , with some looking like Winged Humanoids , others resembling eldritch horrors , and still others being invisible spirits that act In Mysterious Ways . Regardless, when it comes to morality, angels are generally considered Always Lawful Good , with Fallen Angels being formerly good angels who turned evil and were kicked out of heaven. Additionally, it's usually easy to tell if an angel is fallen or not, as the act of falling usually alters an angel's appearances , a common example being good angels being depicted with white wings while evil ones have black wings. However, there are some angels who were never good and are visually indistinguishable from the benevolent angels (if there are any). These angels aren't good but scary , benevolent but ruthless , or good but not nice about it . They are, in short, genuinely evil and malevolent. Even if they follow their own alien form of morality , they are overall more bad than good. Sometimes an angel being a villain is a plot twist, while other times it's present from the beginning. The reason why an angel would be evil without falling can vary. Sometimes it's because God Is Evil , and as a result the angels are evil by extension. Sometimes it's because angels are as morally varied as humans, and are no more inclined to be good than a person is. In a setting with both fallen angels and non-fallen angels it's not uncommon for some fallen angels to be good, or at least not evil, and their reason for falling is either because heaven is corrupt or because the standards for falling are based less on morality and more on following rules. In terms of villainy, they are usually self-righteous in their evil, often suffering from Black-and-White Insanity , believing that their actions are good because they are good and that anyone who disagrees with them is an enemy . In terms of how evil they are, some are Hate Sinks who have no redeeming qualities whatsoever, others have some sympathetic qualities even if they are overall villainous, and still others are Anti Villains whose evil actions are motivated by some kind of good intentions rather than selfishness. In order to qualify for this trope, a character(s) must be an angel/angelic being by the standards of the work in question; Divinely Appearing Demons and other beings that merely resemble angels don't count. mixed descendants of angels can fall under this trope , as can angels who were once mortals . Anti-Hero and Anti-Villain angels can potentially qualify depending on the context. Of course, in order to qualify for this trope, a character must start and stay on the side of evil — an angel that was once good but became evil counts as a Fallen Angel . Often overlaps with Light Is Not Good , Order Is Not Good and Unfeeling Heavens . Related to Our Archons Are Different , in that archons are generally villainous figures who are the Gnostic equivalent of angels. See also Benevolent Demon , where demons are good without having made a Heel–Face Turn , which often overlaps with this trope. Compare/Contrast Divinely Appearing Demons for demons that look angelic. It's entirely possible that an angel described in such terms is the subject of an Unreliable Narrator , particularly if the person describing them is a demonic entity.
